segments.text: Do not judge, or you will be judged.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For with the same judgment you pronounce, you will be judged; and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Why do you look at the speck in your brother’s eye, but fail to notice the beam in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ while there is still a beam in your own eye?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: You hypocrite! First take the beam out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
Do not give dogs what is holy; do not throw your pearls before swine. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and then turn and tear you to pieces.

## Ask, Seek, Knock
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and the door will be opened to you.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: For everyone who asks receives; he who seeks finds; and to him who knocks, the door will be opened.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Which of you, if his son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So if you who are evil know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good things to those who ask Him!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
In everything, then, do to others as you would have them do to you. For this is the essence of the Law and the Prophets.

## The Narrow Gate
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the way that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text:
But small is the gate and narrow the way that leads to life, and only a few find it.

## A Tree and Its Fruit
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Beware of false prophets. They come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ravenous wolves.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: By their fruit you will recognize them. Are grapes gathered from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: So then, by their fruit you will recognize them.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Not everyone who says to Me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only he who does the will of My Father in heaven.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Many will say to Me on that day, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in Your name, and in Your name drive out demons and perform many miracles?’
segments.speaker: Many

segments.text:
Then I will tell them plainly, ‘I never knew you; depart from Me, you workers of lawlessness!’

## The House on the Rock
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Therefore everyone who hears these words of Mine and acts on them is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain fell, the torrents raged, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because its foundation was on the rock.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: But everyone who hears these words of Mine and does not act on them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: The rain fell, the torrents raged, and the winds blew and beat against that house, and it fell—and great was its collapse!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were astonished at His teaching,
because He taught as one who had authority, and not as their scribes.
